Definition.text: Recover is resource restoration (life, mana, or energy shield) applied as a gain effect.

Purpose.text: Restore a depleted resource value rather than increase it permanently.

Core Rules.text: Recover is one member of Recovery forms, alongside regeneration, leech, recoup, and flask effects.

Internal Rules.text: Recovery is pooled by resource; broad recovery-rate modifiers apply to pooled totals and can stack with more specific per-source rates.

Calculation Order.text: After a recovery source triggers, value is added through recovery pool calculations before final per-resource caps are enforced.

Priority Rules.text: Specific recovery source caps and restrictions on pools override generic pool-wide recovery rate modifiers.

Preconditions.text: A gain event on a resource and a valid recipient resource pool.

Postconditions.text: Resource value increases by the recovery amount and may be bounded by current cap or pool-level limits.

Limitations.text: Recovery behavior depends on explicit wording (e.g., on hit, on kill, rate-limited recoup).

Caps.text: The opposite of recovery is loss; recovery is commonly limited by pool caps and source-specific rate caps.

Formula.text: resource_after = min(pool_cap, resource_before + recovered_amount) unless the specific source defines different restrictions.

text: Specific source wording can disable general recovery behavior while active (e.g., cannot gain Life during effect)
Life/ES leech interaction and source-specific historical changes are not merged in this pass.

Hidden Rules.text: Some effects use wording "Recover" but are implemented as other specific mechanics (for example, recoup over time).

Developer Notes.text: Record Recover as generic category with mandatory source-specific exception branch for precise behavior.

Historical Changes.text: No explicit Recover patch timeline captured in this pass.

text.note: Current pooled-recovery interpretation with source-specific caps and rates.

text: Recover 1% Life on Kill restores life immediately once per enemy kill event.
Gain on hit or recoup examples are separate subclasses under the same Recovery umbrella.
